event: Saint Augustine and the Child on the Beach
symbols: bishop's mitre and crozier — Augustine's authority as Bishop of Hippo and Doctor of the Church
child on the seashore — the legend of the child who tried to empty the ocean into a hole, teaching Augustine the impossibility of comprehending the Trinity with finite reason
putto bearing a cross above — the cross of Christ as the key to theological understanding
golden cope — the richness of Augustine's theological legacy and the glory of sacred office
ocean and shore — the infinite mystery of God that exceeds all human comprehension
description: A vertical composition showing an elderly bearded man in magnificent golden episcopal vestments including a richly embroidered cope and a bishop's mitre. He leans forward heavily on a tall crozier, his posture suggesting deep thought or weariness. At his feet on what appears to be a sandy beach sits a small nude child. Above, a winged putto holds or hovers near a tall cross or staff. The background suggests sky and possibly water. The brushwork is loose and vigorous, with warm golden tones dominating the figure's vestments against a cooler, atmospheric background.
